hints.text: Long wide **fjords + jagged green mountains rising straight from the water**, rocky ridgelines along the mountainsides; **almost no naturally growing trees**

shared_with.note: Icelandic coastal mountains look similar but less imposing and less green.
shared_with.diff: Greener + more imposing → Faroes; volcanic black gravel → Iceland.

hints.text: Gen4 = **black pickup truck w/ a front antenna** (always blurred); Gen3 = **black car w/ a plain grey roof rack**
hints.uniq_note: No other country with a similar landscape has these cars — Iceland's truck lacks the antenna.

shared_with.note: Iceland's Gen4 truck has NO front antenna (and is red & white).

hints.text: Houses of **wood on an above-ground stone basement** painted a different colour; roofs **metal or turf (grass)**
hints.uniq_note: The two-tone wood-on-stone build with turf roofs is the classic Faroese look.

hints.text: **Faroese** (closest to Icelandic): Á Ð Í Ó Ú Ý Æ + **Ø**

shared_with.note: Icelandic shares most letters but uses É and Þ instead of Ø.

shared_with.diff: Ð/accented vowels → Faroese; flat Denmark vs fjord walls.

hints.text: Bollards = small **wooden sticks painted yellow**, sometimes red on top
Guardrails w/ **yellow reflectors** — no other northern European country has them
hints.uniq_note: Within northern Europe unique; Andorra/Spain/Belgium use yellow too but look nothing alike.

shared_with.note: Andorra also uses yellow guardrail reflectors.
shared_with.diff: Faroese fjords + Ø/Ð → Faroes; high Pyrenees + Catalan → Andorra.

shared_with.note: Spanish A-type guardrails also use yellow reflectors.
shared_with.diff: Faroese fjords + turf roofs → Faroes; Spanish landscape/language → Spain.

shared_with.note: Belgian A-type guardrails also use yellow reflectors.
shared_with.diff: Faroese fjords + turf roofs → Faroes; flat Belgian landscape → Belgium.

hints.text: Directional signs **yellow w/ thin black border + metal frame**

shared_with.note: Very similar yellow signs in Iceland.
shared_with.diff: Ø + turf roofs → Faroes; ð/þ → Iceland.

hints.text: **Tórshavn**: by far the biggest town, the only place w/ buildings **taller than 3 floors**; relatively open landscape, esp. to the east
**Klaksvík** (2nd biggest): narrow **N-S fjord** w/ fairly steep hills
**Wind parks** (3 visible): largest NW of Tórshavn; others NE of Tórshavn + east of Vestmanna; **Kirkjubøur** (southern tip): black houses w/ grass roofs + small church ruin

bullets: Koltur island: narrow low peninsula on the SE tip
Eiðiskollur: extremely low clouds around the peninsula
West of Skopun: wide strait north w/ the main islands behind
Klakkur: two pyramid-shaped mountains south w/ a thin snow layer
Gjógv: village view south w/ snowy mountains behind
Grey boat 'RIB62' w/ people in reflective clothes → en route to Koltur
